definition: Stable unique ID. Never reuse after deletion; mark deprecated instead.
Raw Yahoo field name or derived/inferred field label.
Preferred CSV/database column name, stable across releases.
Endpoint family that exposes the field.
Dot/bracket path to the raw JSON field. Use semicolon-separated paths for shared fields.
Immediate containing JSON object or array.
Observed Yahoo/JSON type, including array and epoch-unit hints.
Recommended CSV export type.
Recommended database type.
Security types or endpoint contexts where field applies.
Functional grouping.
Plain-English meaning.
Units and scale. Critical for timestamps, percentages, and prices.
Enumeration lookup group if applicable.
Expected refresh/update cadence.
Whether null/missing is allowed.
How often field appears: Common, Conditional, Derived, Rare.
Expected schema/value stability.
Observed/Derived/Inferred/Seeded status.
Semicolon-separated symbols used to observe/confirm field.
Market states where observed or to be captured.
Representative value, not investment data.
Known formula or relationship, if any.
Semicolon-separated related fields.
Soft/hard validation rule for parser tests.
Map, Derived, Ignore, Preserve Raw, Child Rows.
How JSON should be flattened to CSV/database rows.
Group for fields with same raw name in different endpoints.
Representative URL pattern or capture URL.
Implementation notes and caveats.
Project release where row first entered.
ISO date last reviewed.
draft, active, deprecated, needs_review.
Rows may use Request Diagnostics or Endpoint Diagnostics for capture/run metadata, not only raw Yahoo financial fields.
